dialogue.utterance: Unit 3 for a priority 1, male patient with chest pain at a dialysis center in [CITY].
Hi, my name is Steve. I'm an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I've been having chest pain for about an hour now, and it's getting worse.
Can you tell me your name and where we are right now?
My name is Robert, and we're at the dialysis clinic.
And can you tell me what day of the week it is?
Tuesday. Can you check my hands and make sure they work?
Breathing appears regular. I am hearing clear bilateral lung sounds.
Mr. Robert, we're going to take your blood pressure now.
Pulse is 92 now. It's strong and regular.
Respirations are 18. They look smooth and even.
Oxygen saturation is 94 now.
Your ECG shows a sinus rhythm.
Robert, can you tell me the date today and who the current president is?
The president is Biden, and today is January the 24th.
You understand correctly what I'm saying, right? Tell me the name of this building again.
The dialysis clinic. I can move my fingers for you.
Can you tell me about the pain in more detail?
It feels like an ache. The pain started in the center of my chest and is 9 out of 10 right now.
On your worst, that's a 9/10. Has the pain changed or moved over time?
It's gotten worse. It hasn't moved, but it's been there for an hour.
When did the pain start exactly?
About an hour ago, while I was waiting for my dialysis appointment.
Has anything made the pain worse? For instance, movement or breathing deeply?
No, just sitting here.
You mentioned it's an ache. Any other words to describe it?
No, just a deep, ... I don't know, like an energetic ache.
Did your doctors say anything about chest pain in the past?
Yes, last time I had chest pain I was diagnosed with an abnormal potassium level and fluid overload.
Are you on any medications currently?
No medications right now. I take dialysis three times a week-Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.
Do you have any allergies we need to know about?
I'm allergic to Tylenol.
What time was your last dialysis treatment today?
That was at 10:30 a.m.
We're going to apply a cardiac monitor. No pain, just a small touch.
EKG shows a sinus rhythm. We've placed the 4-lead monitor now.
We'll now perform a 12-lead ECG for a more detailed reading. May take a moment.
The first 12-lead ECG result shows a nonspecific T wave abnormality and a supraventricular rhythm in a 40+ male.
We've safely completed the second scan with the 12-lead monitor. Findings are unchanged-again showing no STEMI or other acute cardiac features.
You look alert, oriented to self, place, and time. GCS is 15.
You appear fully awake. Eye-opening is spontaneous. Verbal response is appropriate. Motor function is intact.
Your skin looks normal in color, warm to the touch. No rashes or abnormalities noted.
Mr. Robert, I'm going to give you 324 mg of aspirin now. It may help with the pain.
Pulse is now 90 after medication.
Blood pressure is 134 over 70. Overall, stable.
Oxygen saturation has improved, now at 98.
Reassessed vitals: Pulse is 90, Respirations 18, BP is 134/70, SpO2 is 98, EKG is sinus rhythm.
Glucose is not detectable at this time.
We gave a repeat dose of 324 mg aspirin. Let's wait to see if there's any improvement.
Have you noticed the pain changing at all since the medication?
Slightly. It started to feel better while I was being loaded into the ambulance.
Following protocol 2-1 for medical chest pain with cardiac suspicion due to the EKG abnormality and your history of end-stage renal disease.
Mr. Robert, we'll need to move you onto the stretcher now. Safety first.
You're secure and stable. All set for transport to the hospital.
Robert, would you like us to take you to [COMPANY_NAME]?
Yes, [COMPANY_NAME] is fine. I'll go there.
Transport is secured. We will be heading to [COMPANY_NAME]. I've updated the hospital with patient status and history.
Transport status is secure and en route to [COMPANY_NAME] with chest pain concern, EKG abnormality, and pre-existing renal issues.
Pulse at load in vehicle: 90.
Blood pressure is 134 over 70.
Still 18 respirations.
Oxygen saturation remains steady at 98.
En route to [COMPANY_NAME]. Priority cared for chest pain, EKG abnormality, and end-stage renal disease.
Oxygen remains on at 2 liters per minute. No further interventions needed at this time.
Let me check in again-any changes in your pain level?
Still a 9/10 but I feel slightly better than before.
Just to confirm-no medications taken recently, no other allergies besides Tylenol?
No, that's correct. I had dialysis earlier and nothing else was given at the clinic.
We have a full report and medication history for the receiving team. Everything stable at the moment.
Pulse is still at 90. BP is 134 over 70. SpO2 unchanged at 98. No EKG changes reported.
Expect arrival in approximately 15 minutes. No signs of deterioration so far.
Hospital staff are on notice. I've repeated the key concerns and EKG findings in our last report.
